_exempt: <!-- PRIMARY-SOURCE-EXEMPT: reason=QA-KG Phase 4.5+4.6 Iverson primary-source seed. Verbatim quotes from Ben Iverson's QA-1 (1993 OCR), QA-2 (1993), Pyth-3 Enneagram (2006 w/ Carl Elkins), and QA Vol.II Synchronous Harmonics (Feb 1991). Locators resolve against docs/theory/iverson_*_excerpts.md and qa_corpus_text/qa-1__qa_1_all_pages__ocr_full.md. Do not edit quote fields. -->
description: Iverson 13-claim corpus. 4 SourceWorks (QA-1 / QA-2 / Pyth-3 Enneagram / QA Vol.II). QA-1 (Book 1, 1993) added 2026-05-31 from full OCR of QA-1-All-Pages.pdf (84pp, tesseract 5.5.2). Certs [135][137][148] already cited Iverson Pyth-1 without a SourceWork entry; this adds the missing work + 7 claims with derived_from edges. domain=qa_core on all. Claim sc:iverson_pyth3_fermat_3d_cubes carries a confidence_override on the stitched two-sentence paraphrase span.

works.title: Iverson — Pythagoras and the Quantum World, Volume 1 / Quantum Arithmetic Book 1 (1993)
works.body: Ben Iverson, Pythagoras and the Quantum World, Volume 1 — also titled Quantum Arithmetic Book 1. Published by ITAM / Delta Spectrum Research, Portland OR, September 1993. ISBN 1-883401-06-2 (individual; OCR reads 1-663401-06-2 — likely print/scan artifact on '8'→'6'). First book in Iverson's QA textbook series. Self-described as 'the origination of Quantum Arithmetic' (page 57 of PDF). Chapters: Fibonacci/Pythagoras/Primes (Ch1), Prime Pythagorean Triangles (Ch2), Algebra (Ch3), Plato & A'hmose (Ch4), Koenig Series (Ch5), Ellipse of Archimedes (Ch6), History (Ch7), plus Problems & Questions and Index. Source of QA core definitions: Quantum Number, par numbers, Koenig Series, algebraic formula set (d=b+e, a=b+2e, G=d²+e², etc.), Egyptian fraction unit fractions, female QA numbers.
works.confidence_override_reason: OCR quality verified: tesseract 5.5.2 at 200dpi-png, <15 non-ASCII chars per text-heavy page, sentences coherent throughout all 84 pages. Confidence 0.95 reflects high OCR fidelity on well-printed 1993 typeset text.

works.title: Iverson — Quantum Arithmetic Book 2: Natural Arithmetic (1993)
works.body: Ben Iverson, Quantum Arithmetic Book 2: Natural Arithmetic. Published by ITAM / Delta Spectrum Research, La Junta CO. November 1993. ISBN 1-883401-07-0 (individual); 1-883401-05-4 (4-volume set). Second book in Iverson's QA textbook series — covers doubling, double parity, the Quantum Ellipse, slicing the ellipse, Synchronous Harmonics, and equilateral/isosceles triangles.

works.title: Iverson & Elkins — Pythagoras and the Quantum World Volume III: Enneagram (2006)
works.body: Ben Iverson & Carl Elkins, Pythagoras and the Quantum World Volume III: Enneagram. Published by Delta Spectrum Research, 921 Santa Fe Avenue, La Junta CO. 2006. ESBN 1-883401-03-0. Third volume in the Pythagoras series; extends QA with the 9-pointed enneagram and Fermat-style 3D cube identities.

works.title: Iverson — QA Volume II Books 3 & 4: New Wave Theory — Synchronous Harmonics (1991/2005)
works.body: Ben Iverson, Quantum Arithmetic Volume II: Books 3 & 4 — New Wave Theory / Synchronous Harmonics. Originally February 1991 ('long lost book — now found 11/10/05'). Republished by Delta Spectrum Research, 921 Santa Fé Avenue, La Junta CO, 2005. Dynamic phase of QA; Synchronous Harmonics model where wavelets combine via the seven prime numbers in a Quantum Number.

claims.quote: A QUANTUM NUMBER consists of four numbers, two of which are Co-prime, along with their sum and their difference - Defined as follows: (a), The sum and the difference will be prime to the middle two,(Euclid); Or (b), In every Quantum Number, the sum of the first and second equals the third, and the sum of the second and third equals the fourth.
claims.title: Iverson QA-1 p.15: Quantum Number = four numbers, two coprime + sum + difference (b,e,d,a)
claims.confidence_override_reason: OCR quality verified: tesseract 5.5.2 at 200dpi-png, <15 non-ASCII chars per text-heavy page, sentences coherent throughout all 84 pages. Confidence 0.95 reflects high OCR fidelity on well-printed 1993 typeset text.

claims.quote: d=b+e; a=b+2e; A=a²; B=b²; D=d²; E=e²; C=2de; F=ab; G=d²+e²=D+E; L=abde/6=CF/12; H=C+F; I=C-F (Absolute); J=db; K=da
claims.title: Iverson QA-1 p.29 Ch3 Algebra: complete QA algebraic formula set (d=b+e, G=D+E, L=CF/12, H=C+F, I=C-F)
claims.confidence_override_reason: OCR quality verified: tesseract 5.5.2 at 200dpi-png, <15 non-ASCII chars per text-heavy page, sentences coherent throughout all 84 pages. Confidence 0.95 reflects high OCR fidelity on well-printed 1993 typeset text.

claims.quote: The Koenig Series is important for the reason that it opens the way for mathematical relationships to show what a Quantum Number really is, and how it is applied. In the numbers of the Koenig series, it shows how one gets from the root numbers to quantum linear distances, to quantum area measurements.
claims.title: Iverson QA-1 p.38 Ch5: Koenig Series opens root→linear→area measurement chain
claims.confidence_override_reason: OCR quality verified: tesseract 5.5.2 at 200dpi-png, <15 non-ASCII chars per text-heavy page, sentences coherent throughout all 84 pages. Confidence 0.95 reflects high OCR fidelity on well-printed 1993 typeset text.

claims.quote: The first set of circles, (1, 5, 7 radii,) fits inside the second set of circles, (7, 13, 17, radii). This is the beginning of one branch of the Koenig Series. This branch can be continued with the 24, 7, 25 triangle. Now the I, G, H values will be I=24-7=17; G=25; and H=24+7=31.
claims.title: Iverson QA-1 p.39 Ch5: Koenig nested circle radii (1,5,7)→(7,13,17)→(17,25,31); I=H_prev
claims.confidence_override_reason: OCR quality verified: tesseract 5.5.2 at 200dpi-png, <15 non-ASCII chars per text-heavy page, sentences coherent throughout all 84 pages. Confidence 0.95 reflects high OCR fidelity on well-printed 1993 typeset text.

claims.quote: 2/97 = 1/(7X8) + 1/(7x97) + 1/(8x97), and the fraction is written: 2/97 = 1/56 + 1/679 + 1/776. ... the Rhind Mathematical Papyrus (RMP) uses the fraction 1/60 + 1/291 + 1/1940.
claims.title: Iverson QA-1 p.11 Ch1: 2/97 unit fraction via QA Quantum Number; RMP uses 1/60+1/291+1/1940
claims.confidence_override_reason: OCR quality verified: tesseract 5.5.2 at 200dpi-png, <15 non-ASCII chars per text-heavy page, sentences coherent throughout all 84 pages. Confidence 0.95 reflects high OCR fidelity on well-printed 1993 typeset text.

claims.quote: the female number can be derived directly from the male number. It is done by doubling the two intermediate numbers of the male Quantum Number and placing them at the two ends, for the female Quantum Number. In doing so, the first added to the second will equal the third. And the second added to the third is equal to the fourth.
claims.title: Iverson QA-1 p.27 Ch2: female QA number derived by doubling male's middle two numbers to outer positions
claims.confidence_override_reason: OCR quality verified: tesseract 5.5.2 at 200dpi-png, <15 non-ASCII chars per text-heavy page, sentences coherent throughout all 84 pages. Confidence 0.95 reflects high OCR fidelity on well-printed 1993 typeset text.

claims.quote: A Quantum Number is a set of four numbers like those with which Fibonacci worked.
claims.title: Iverson QA-1 p.12 Ch1: Quantum Number = four-number set identical to Fibonacci's structure
claims.confidence_override_reason: OCR quality verified: tesseract 5.5.2 at 200dpi-png, <15 non-ASCII chars per text-heavy page, sentences coherent throughout all 84 pages. Confidence 0.95 reflects high OCR fidelity on well-printed 1993 typeset text.

claims.quote: Quantum Arithmetic is not a replacement for conventional mathematics courses, however it does replace most Trigonometry, some Algebra, and all Calculus.
claims.title: Iverson QA-2 (1993): QA replaces most trig, some algebra, all calculus

claims.quote: If the first integer is 2-par, the fourth integer will be 4-par. If the first integer is 4-par, then the fourth will be 2-par.
claims.title: Iverson QA-2 Ch.1 Doubling: female-root 2-par / 4-par parity law

claims.quote: The Quantum Number for the orbit of Halley's Comet is 1, 29, 30, 59. Its ratio between the perigee and apogee is 1:59. This is a much elongated elliptical orbit.
claims.title: Iverson QA-2 Ch.1 Doubling: Halley's Comet QN=(1,29,30,59)

claims.quote: All trigonometric formulae are transposable into any trigonometric function. Tan=F/C, or Tan=ab/2de. Sin=F/G or Sin=ab/(d²+e²) = b.
claims.title: Iverson Pyth-3 Introduction: Tan=F/C, Sin=F/G QA identity transposition

claims.quote: In the case of 'Fermat's Last Theorem', the sum of two squares, one even and one odd, will be an odd square. The extension of this to third dimension is 'The sum of three cubes can be equal to the cube.' (3²+4²=5²) and (3³+4³+5³=6³)
claims.confidence_override_reason: stitched two-sentence paraphrase span: source prints the Fermat framing and the 3D extension on consecutive but non-contiguous lines; quoted material is verbatim but assembled into one claim body for readability. Flagged for re-extraction in Phase 4.6 with a line-accurate locator.
claims.title: Iverson Pyth-3 Introduction: Fermat sum-of-squares extended to 3D cubes

claims.quote: Quantum Arithmetic - Volume II / Books 3 & 4 / New Wave Theory - Synchronous Harmonics / by Ben Iverson / February, 1991 / (long lost book - now found 11/10/05)
claims.title: Iverson QA Vol.II title page: Synchronous Harmonics naming (1991/2005)

derived_from.rationale: Cert [148] certifies the 16 named quantities and 9 algebraic relations of a prime Pythagorean direction; the Quantum Number four-element coprime+sum+difference definition from QA-1 p.15 is the foundational premise those identities build on.
Cert [148] certifies G+C=A, G-C=B, F²+C²=G², L=CF/12, H=C+F, I=C-F, W=X+K; QA-1 p.29 Ch3 is the primary source for exactly those formulas including L=abde/6=CF/12 and H=C+F, I=C-F.
Cert [137] certifies H²-G²=G²-I²=2CF=24L; the H=C+F and I=C-F definitions from QA-1 p.29 are the direct algebraic premises for H²-I²=4CF.
Cert [135] certifies M_A/M_B/M_C generator moves = Koenig tree navigation; QA-1 p.38 establishes the Koenig Series as the framework those moves traverse.
Cert qa_koenig_closure certifies closure properties of the Koenig series; QA-1 p.38 is the primary source establishing what the Koenig series is and why it matters.
QA-1 p.39 gives the concrete (1,5,7)→(7,13,17)→(17,25,31) circle sequence that the Koenig closure cert generalizes; this is the primary-source witness for the iterative nesting structure.
Cert [134] certifies the Koenig descent = Egyptian fraction steps and the RMP greedy algorithm; QA-1 p.11 gives the 2/97 unit fraction decomposition from the RMP as the named primary-source example.
Cert [144] certifies male-female octave relations; QA-1 p.27 is the primary source for the female QN derivation rule (double the middle two males, place at outer positions) that the cert formalizes.

Cert [282] certifies the mod-9 orbit class of F_n via Fibonacci index; QA-1 p.12 establishes Fibonacci numbers and QA Quantum Numbers as the same structural object, grounding the connection between Fibonacci recurrence and QA orbit periods.
